commit.message:
fix(model-3d): fix splitFace origins, extrude wall winding, and orphan cleanup

- Fix splitFace: swap split HE origins (endV→startV for face A, startV→endV for face B)
  to prevent duplicate vertices in face loops causing degenerate triangulation
- Fix extrude Case 2/3: remove direction-dependent wall winding ternary, always use
  [oldA, oldB, newB, newA] for consistent twin relationships
- Add detachBoundaryHE for cleaning up orphaned boundary HEs after extrude Case 3
- Unlink boundary HEs from chain before deletion in addFace to prevent stale pointers
- Add stricter validate(): twin origin consistency + duplicate vertex detection

All 64 kernel tests pass.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix(model-3d): fix face splitting — snap line to plane + use triangle lookup

Three fixes for face splitting:
1. Snap line endpoints onto face plane before 2D projection (fixes
   floating-point errors from matrixWorld.invert() causing missed
   intersections)
2. Use triangleToHEFace for O(1) face lookup instead of findFaceAtPoint
   (avoids point-in-polygon edge cases at vertices/edges)
3. Include updatedAt in edge geometry key so edges re-render after splits

commit.message:
chore(release): add vendored source archive to goreleaser (#644)

## Summary

- Add `source` section to `.goreleaser.yaml` to produce a vendored
source archive (`micasa-<version>.tar.gz`) on each release
- Run `go mod vendor` via a global `before.hooks` step so the `vendor/`
directory is available for the archive
- Set `prefix_template` so extraction creates a containing
`micasa-<version>/` directory
- Include `vendor/**` in the source archive for offline builds

closes #642

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix(fortress): 포탄 두 발 스킬이 3발 나가던 버그 수정

메인 1발 + 추가 2발(±7도) = 3발이었던 것을
메인 1발 + 추가 1발(+7도) = 2발로 수정

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix: reserve 4 ports per OpenClaw agent instead of 3

OpenClaw binds 4 ports per gateway instance: gateway (+0), internal
(+1), control service (+2), and CDP relay (+3). We were only reserving
3 ports, so the 2nd agent's gateway port (base+3) collided with the
1st agent's CDP relay port (base+3), causing "port already in use"
crash-loops for every agent after the first.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Tests updated:
- test_auth_routes.py: update bootstrap sub assertion to new valid UUID
- test_chat_routes.py: update feedback message_id to valid UUID format

All 785 backend tests pass. Frontend: all platform admin screens render
with correct layout, dark theme (#0c0e14), sidebar navigation, and zero
unexpected 500 errors from authenticated API calls.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

L0 prompts: require source context in trait descriptions

Traits should read like facts with provenance, not AI summaries.

Before: "以尼采哲学为核心，将'Why to live'作为生存准则"
After:  "在一封写给孩子的信中，引用尼采'He who has a why to live
        can almost bear anyhow'，以此建立生活准则——戒酒、保持健康、
        活到孩子40岁。"

Updated both analyze_content.md and consolidate.md with new examples
and explicit "带出处" rule for all trait types.

Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.ai/code)
via [Happy](https://happy.engineering)

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
Co-Authored-By: Happy <yesreply@happy.engineering>

commit.message:
feat(logger): replace mode picker dropdown with keyboard-navigable list

Swap the F3 mode picker from a Select widget with OK/Cancel buttons to a
compact ListView. Arrow keys or Tab navigate modes; Enter selects; Escape
cancels. No buttons needed. Popup sized to fit the list only.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix: duplicação no resumo financeiro (cross-join na agregação)

A query de agregação referenciava TrocaOleo.id/valor_total diretamente
enquanto usava base.subquery() no select_from, causando cross-join
implícito que dobrava todos os valores do resumo.

Corrigido usando sub.c.* (colunas da subquery) ao invés de TrocaOleo.*.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Add on-device audio intelligence pipeline (#35) (#35)

New `bolo-audio` crate with mic capture, VAD, and sound classification.
Ported from bolo_march4_2026, replacing MQTT with BoloEvent broadcast.

- CPAL mic capture → resample to 32kHz → energy gate → Silero VAD → EfficientAT classifier
- Auto-downloads ONNX models (~41MB) from GitHub release on first use
- New `DownloadManager::ensure_file()` for generic URL-to-file downloads
- AudioEngine with spawn_blocking for ONNX model loading (non-blocking UI)
- Arc-wrapped AudioEngine + DownloadManager to release NodeState lock during startup
- Tauri commands: start_audio, stop_audio, get_audio_status, list_audio_devices
- Audio events emitted as direct payloads for clean frontend consumption
- SolidJS Audio page with live energy meter, VAD indicator, top-5 classification bars, rolling history
- 20 unit tests across spectrogram, preprocessing, VAD, speech buffer modules

Co-authored-by: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

feat: analytics dashboard — journeys+assets data fix, marketing insights engine

- Rewrite JOURNEYS config: 4→9 journeys (all real funnels), step-based with metric type
- Fix trailing slash mismatch: all paths now match DB format (/academy/ not /academy)
- Fix assets tab: 12 digital assets with correct path matching
- Remove references to non-existent form_start/form_abandon events
- Build marketing insights engine: 11 automated analyses (campaigns, journeys, conversions, timing, device gaps)
- Replace weak CRO suggestions with deep data-driven recommendations
- Auto-load advanced data for insights tab

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

feat: translocation_dashboard - add GCF translocation success tracking

Adds translocation_success_tab() function that:
- Filters to GCF-affiliated translocations (organisation_1/2/3 check)
- Matches mortality events via gir_mortality_trans_serial field
- Computes 1-month survival (translocation success) and 1-year survival (short-term success)
- Shows "Pending" for events too recent to assess
- No linked mortality = assumed 100% survival
- Summary metrics + colour-coded per-translocation table + CSV download
- Appended at bottom of translocation dashboard view

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Realign apocrypha reading plan to BibleHub chronology day-by-day

Restructure the 365-day apocrypha/pseudepigrapha reading plan to serve
as supplementary study material aligned with each canonical period:

- Days 1-14: Primeval texts (Adam & Eve, Enoch) with Creation/Job
- Days 15-31: Patriarchal texts with Abraham/Isaac/Jacob/Joseph
- Days 32-75: Exodus/Wilderness texts with Torah
- Days 76-102: Conquest/Judges texts with Joshua/Judges
- Days 103-158: Davidic/Solomonic wisdom with Samuel/Psalms
- Days 159-206: Maccabean/Prophetic texts with Kingdom period
- Days 207-280: Exilic/Restoration texts with Prophets
- Days 281-330: Apostolic Fathers with Gospels/Acts
- Days 331-365: NT Apocrypha with Epistles/Revelation

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
